summary: Decompiled two small functions: `IsNameUnique` in the name-entry/menu name system and `un_80305D00` in toy input code. The PR replaced nonmatching placeholders with C implementations and tightened the `IsNameUnique` header prototype from unknown return/params to `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`. The automated match report credited 2 new 100% matches, +316 matched bytes total. The PR body excerpt appears inconsistent with the actual diff, describing unrelated stage functions, so the reliable evidence is the title, diff, changed files, and match report.
what_changed: `src/melee/mn/mnname.c`: replaced `/// #IsNameUnique` with a matching implementation of `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`.

`IsNameUnique` loops over up to `0x78` name slots, checks validity with `IsNameValid(i & 0xFF)`, obtains `GetPersistentNameData((u8) i)->namedata` for valid entries, stops when `namedata == NULL`, and returns `true` when `CompareNameStrings(namedata, slot) == 0`; otherwise returns `false`.
`src/melee/mn/mnname.h`: updated the declaration from `UNK_RET IsNameUnique(UNK_PARAMS)` to `bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot)`.
`src/melee/ty/toy.c`: replaced `/// #un_80305D00` with a matching implementation returning a `float` stick-X value.

`un_80305D00` scans four pads via `HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8) i].nml_stickX`, keeps the signed stick value in `ret`, compares the absolute value against `0.1F`, breaks on the first pad beyond the threshold, and returns the signed value.
Automation reported 2 new full matches: `main/melee/ty/toy` `un_80305D00` at +176 bytes and `main/melee/mn/mnname` `IsNameUnique` at +140 bytes.
smart_moves: Preserved signed return semantics in `un_80305D00` by assigning `ret = val` before taking the absolute value for threshold testing.
Used explicit `(u8)` casts in both functions where the underlying code appears to index or pass byte-sized slot/pad values, helping match codegen.
Updated the public header prototype for `IsNameUnique` at the same time as the implementation, reducing future reliance on `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS`.
Modeled `IsNameUnique` with a `namedata == NULL` early break rather than scanning all 0x78 entries unconditionally, matching the observed control flow.
decomp_lessons: For name-system functions, preserve byte truncation in loop indices when existing callees or data formats expect `u8` slots, even if the loop variable is an `int`.
When matching analog-stick helper logic, separate the signed value returned from the absolute value used for deadzone comparison.
Small decomp PRs should include header cleanup when a function signature becomes known; replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` is part of the decomp win.

Be cautious with PR body text in generated dumps: this slice's summary mentions unrelated `grIceMt_801F91EC` and `grKongo_801D8078`, while the diff and match report clearly concern `IsNameUnique` and `un_80305D00`.
naming_conventions: `IsNameUnique` uses PascalCase-style naming already present in `mnname` APIs such as `IsNameListFull`, `CompareNameStrings`, `GetPersistentNameData`, `DeleteName`, `IsNameValid`, and `CreateNameAtIndex`.
The parameter name `slot` was chosen for `IsNameUnique`, consistent with nearby name-entry functions that operate on name slots.
`un_80305D00` remained unnamed with its address-based `un_` symbol, indicating the PR matched behavior without claiming a semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `int i` loop counters with explicit `(u8)` casts at call/index sites to reproduce byte truncation while keeping normal C loop structure.
For float absolute-value comparisons, a manual `if (val < 0.0F) { val = -val; }` pattern can match codegen while retaining the original signed value separately.
A threshold literal of `0.1F` was used for the stick deadzone check in `un_80305D00`.
The name loop bound `0x78` and `i & 0xFF` expression were preserved exactly in `IsNameUnique`, suggesting these forms were important for matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
